title: Luscious Malaysian Pork Tenderloin Bao
description: Yeasty, silky bao filled with savory pork tenderloin, dairy-free and brunch‑ready.
instructions: Trim the pork tenderloin into a 500g portion and slice into 1cm thick medallions.
In a bowl, whisk together coconut aminos, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and five-spice powder until sugar dissolves.
Coat the pork slices with the mixture, cover, and marinate in the fridge for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare a slurry by mixing cornstarch and water until smooth, then set aside.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and sear the marinated pork for 2–3 minutes per side until caramelized.
Add the cornstarch slurry to the pan, stir until the sauce thickens, then remove from heat.
Warm the vegan bao buns in a steamer for 3 minutes, then fill each bun with 80–100g of the pork and a sprinkle of sesame seeds.
Serve the bao hot with a side of fresh cucumber slices for a refreshing finish.
